data.title: Stevanato Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Stevanato Group press release (NYSE:STVN): Q4 Revenue for the fourth quarter of 2024 increased 3% to €330.6 million, and high-value solutions represented 40% of total revenue, in line with Company expectations.
  • For the fourth quarter, diluted earnings per share were €0.18, and adjusted diluted earnings per share were €0.19.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin increased 50 basis points to 27.5% for the fourth quarter.
  • The Company is establishing fiscal year 2025 guidance and expects revenue in the range of €1,160 million to €1,190 million, adjusted EBITDA in the range of €293.0 million to €306.3 million, and adjusted diluted EPS between €0.51 and €0.55.

data.title: Guild Holdings declares $0.50 dividend
data.description:
• Guild Holdings (NYSE:GHLD) declares $0.50/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 15.27%
  • Payable March 31; for shareholders of record March 17; ex-div March 17.
  • See GHLD Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Tejon Ranch GAAP EPS of $0.17, revenue of $17.94M
data.description:
• Tejon Ranch press release (NYSE:TRC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.17.
  • Revenue of $17.94M (+10.9% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, increased 116% to $10.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024 compared to $4.8 million for the same period in 2023.

  • 2025 Outlook:

    The Company will continue to opportunistically pursue commercial/industrial development, multi-family development, leasing, sales, and investment within TRCC and its joint ventures. The Company also will continue to invest in its residential projects, including Mountain Village at Tejon Ranch, Centennial at Tejon Ranch and Grapevine at Tejon Ranch.

    California is one of the most highly regulated states in which to engage in real estate development and, as such, natural delays, including those resulting from litigation, can be reasonably anticipated. Accordingly, throughout the next few years, the Company expects net income to fluctuate year-to-year based on the above-mentioned activity, along with commodity prices, production within its farming and mineral resources segments, and the timing of land sales and leasing of land within its commercial developments.

    Water sales opportunities each year are impacted by the total precipitation and snowpack runoff in Northern California from winter storms along with State Water Project, or SWP, allocations. The current SWP allocation is at 35% of contract amounts as of February 25, 2025. Although the allocation may increase, the Company is optimistic for a year-over-year increase in water sales opportunities in 2025.

    The Company expects its 2025 farming operations to continue to be impacted by higher costs of production, such as fuel costs, fertilizer costs, pest control costs, and labor costs. The almond industry currently projects 2024 yields to be about 2.6 billion pounds, down from the previous report of over 3.0 billion pounds. The Company expects this estimate, along with a lower inventory carry forward, will help improve pricing. Additionally in 2025, the Company's crop segmentation in its farming division will include the planting of an olive orchard, diversifying the Company's commodity products and best positioning the Company for market changes.

data.title: Affinity Bancshares adopts buyback program to repurchase up to 320,480 shares
data.description:
• Affinity Bancshares (NASDAQ:AFBI) has adopted a stock repurchase program where it may repurchase up to 320,480 shares of its common stock, or approximately 5% of the current outstanding shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wayfair upgraded at Jefferies; cuts technology workforce
data.description:
Thanks to an improved housing market, business-to-business traction, and an underrated physical retail expansion, Wayfair (NYSE:W) is at a compelling valuation, trading at a discount to peers despite projected profit growth that is nearly 3x its peers through 2027.

“Our call is simple,” Jefferies analyst Jonathan Matuszewski says in his research note, “Wayfair has averaged a ~42% premium versus peers since COVID ended, and today, Wayfair trades at an 8% discount.”

Matuszewski cites the momentum in the housing market, specifically out West; top-tier share gains; the company’s new loyalty program; and, its physical presence with brick-and-mortar stores amplifying the digital business, as catalysts for his upgrade to Buy from Hold and a new target price that is a 42% premium to Thursday’s closing price.

Jefferies’ analysis of ~38 home furnishing peers shows steady outperformance through the third quarter of 2024 with Wayfair (NYSE:W) registering the highest market share growth since 2021. And recognizing that demand for Wayfair (NYSE:W) is more cyclical than other consumer internet peers, Matuszewski views a high-single-digit percentage premium reasonable given Wayfair’s (NYSE:W) outsized EBITDA growth.

Making the argument for Wayfair’s (W) attractive valuation, Matuszewski points to Wayfair’s (W) performance twenty-four hours after COVID was declared a global pandemic. The stock fell 27% to close at $33. Today, Wayfair (W) is trading at that level despite a revenue base that is 30% larger, EBITDA margins that have flipped to mid-single-digit growth, and positive free cash flow.

“Bottom-line, at 14.2x 2025 EBITDA, we view the medium-term growth algorithm of double-digit percentage sales growth and a path towards double-digit percentage EBITDA margins attractive,” Matuszewski says.

Separately, Wayfair (W) announced on Friday a workforce reduction of approximately 340 members of the technology team after completing modernization and re-platforming initiatives.

As a result of this reorganization, Wayfair (W) expects to incur charges of ~$33M to $38M in employee severance costs. These costs will largely offset structural cost savings, but savings won’t be realized until the second half of 2025.

Wayfair (W) shares are 2% higher in Friday’s premarket trade after setting a two-year low on Thursday.

data.title: Trulieve appoints Ryan Blust as interim chief financial officer
data.description:
• Trulieve Cannabis (TCNNF) has appointed Ryan Blust, the company's vice president, finance, as its interim chief financial officer, effective March 6, 2025.
  • The company's former CFO Wes Getman departed to pursue other opportunities, Trulieve added.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: I-Mab rises after update on early-stage trial for gastric cancer therapy
data.description:
I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB) ADRs traded ~11% higher in the premarket after the biotech indicated a faster-than-expected advancement in its Phase 1b trial for its experimental gastric cancer therapy, givastomig.

The early-stage trial is designed to evaluate the bispecific antibody in combination with Bristol Myers Squibb’s (BMY) anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor, nivolumab, and chemotherapy as a first-line option for gastric cancer.

After completing enrollments in the dose escalation section of the trial, the investigators have already completed the enrollments for the first dose expansion cohort ahead of schedule, Rockville, Maryland-based I-Mab (NASDAQ:IMAB).

“Enrollment in the first dose expansion cohort was completed months ahead of schedule, and we are observing a similar rate of recruitment in the second expansion cohort,” IMAB’s Chief Medical Officer, Phillip Dennis, said.

Initial results from the 40-patient dose expansion and dose escalation portions of the study are expected in H1 2026 and H2 2025, respectively.

data.title: Wolfspeed to reduce headcount by about 180 employees
data.description:
• Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) said in a filing on Friday that it is reducing its headcount by approximately 180 employees, primarily in material operations, at both the Durham and Siler City locations.
  • In addition, in the third quarter of FY2025, the company expects to take a one-time charge relating to a litigation settlement.
  • Finally, the company announced that it has received its conditional Certificate of Occupancy for the Siler City Materials Facility.

data.title: Acurx Pharmaceuticals stock sinks on $1.1M direct offering
data.description:
• Acurx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ACXP) stock tumbled 36.4% in premarket trade after it said it was looking to raise $1.1M through a direct offering of 2.75M shares of its common stock.
  • The offering is priced at $0.40 apiece, which is at a discount of 38.7% as compared to its last close on Thursday.
  • Additionally, in a concurrent private placement, the company will issue unregistered short-term warrants to purchase up to 8.24M shares, and the warrants will have an exercise price of $0.40 apiece. 
  • The firm expects to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: African Rainbow Minerals Non-GAAP EPS of R 7.75, revenue of R 6.38B
data.description:
• African Rainbow Minerals press release (OTCPK:AFBOF): 1H Non-GAAP EPS of R 7.75.
  • Revenue of R 6.38B (-3.3% Y/Y).
  • An interim dividend of R4.50 per share is declared (1H F2024: R6.00 per share).
  • It maintained a robust financial position, with net cash of R6 073 million at 31 December 2024 (30 June 2024: R7 197 million).
